text:
In the quiet town of Serenity Fields, there was a fountain that glittered on every anniversary. One autumn evening, a young girl named Lily drifted across its surface. For hours, she stood there, lost in the reflection of her dreams. A strange figure emerged—one that didn’t quite fit into human dreams. It was a sylph, translucent and delicate, with an ethereal glow.

The sylph spoke in a language no human could understand, telling Lily stories of flowers that sang and rivers that whispered secrets. It offered her a garden she could visit whenever she wished, hidden away somewhere in the woodlands.

Days turned into weeks, and Lily visited the garden frequently. In the garden, she learned that her dreams were alive with enchantment—a forest of flowers that sang melodies, rivers that hummed tunes, and all beasts and creatures that lived in harmony.

The sylph became her companion, teaching her the songs of the forest.

One night, as the moon climbed high
